sentence: Subjective:
HPI: Maria Martin, a 74-year-old female, presents for follow-up.
She reports her sugars are not well controlled, with an A1C of 9.9.
She has been taking glimepiride 2mg once a day, but has not increased the dose as previously instructed.
She has been trying to eat healthier, but admits to consuming wheat bread and occasionally peanut butter sandwiches.
She drinks club soda and seltzer water, and has not had any regular water.
She has had cataract surgery and has been experiencing peripheral neuropathy in her legs, which causes pain up to her hips.
She also reports feeling like her feet are "like hell." She has a history of high blood pressure, high cholesterol, and vascular disease in her legs.
She is currently taking metoprolol, lisinopril, hydrochlorothiazide, amlodipine, and atorvastatin.
She has a history of smoking and has not attempted to quit.
Past medical history: She has a history of cataracts, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, and vascular disease in her legs.
She has undergone cataract surgery and has been prescribed glasses.
Review of systems: She denies headaches, dizziness, shortness of breath, or chest pains.
She reports occasional coughing due to congestion, but no fever, chills, or nausea.
She experiences urinary frequency, with a slower stream but stable, and urinates several times per day.
Current medications:
Glimepiride 2mg once a day
Metoprolol 50mg once a day
Lisinopril 20mg/ hydrochlorothiazide 25mg once a day
Amlodipine 10mg once a day
Atorvastatin (dose not specified)
Objective:
Vital signs: Blood pressure 140/60, pulse rate not mentioned, oxygen saturation not mentioned, temperature not mentioned.
Physical exam: Carotids are normal, pulse is not mentioned, heart exam is regular with a normal rate, S1 and S2 are crisp, no murmurs are present.
Lungs are clear, with no rasps or wheezing.
Extremities are without edema, pulses are palpable on the dorsalis pedis posterior tibial.
She has thickened nails, mildly dry skin, and no skin breakdown.
She has mildly decreased sensation in the tips of the first and second toes.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has poorly controlled diabetes mellitus with an A1C of 9.9, high blood pressure, high cholesterol, and peripheral neuropathy in her legs.
Problem list: Diabetes mellitus; high blood pressure; high cholesterol; peripheral neuropathy; vascular disease in the legs.
Plan:
I prescribed glimepiride 4mg twice a day to improve blood sugar control.
I instructed the patient to take two of the pills she has at home twice a day until she receives the new prescription.
I will continue the metformin.
I will recheck her blood pressure and kidney function today.
I will continue the combination of medications for her high blood pressure and high cholesterol.
I advised the patient to quit smoking, but she is not ready to quit.
Follow-up: I will see the patient back in four months to recheck her blood sugar, blood pressure, and kidney function.
